question_title: Will the interest in “jake paul” change between 2026-03-16 and 2026-03-24 according to Google Trends?
summary:
Jake Paul search interest spiked to 100 on Mar 12 (driven by Trump endorsement/political office news) and has been decaying: 100→39→25→15→13 by Mar 16. The start value is 13. "Doesn't change" covers 10-16, "Decreases" requires <10, and "Increases" requires >16.

The core tension is whether interest settles at 10-12 (slightly elevated due to the ongoing political narrative) or returns to the pre-spike baseline of 7-8 (which would trigger Decreases). The decay rate is clearly slowing (ratio 0.87 on the last day), and the Trump endorsement / "running for office" story is a developing narrative with massive rising queries, which could sustain interest above the 7-8 floor. However, 8 more days of gradual decay without a new catalyst could bring the value below 10. No major Jake Paul events are scheduled in the window, and he's recovering from jaw surgery. Increases is unlikely without a significant new catalyst. Monte Carlo simulation with mean-reverting dynamics produced ~9% Increases, ~55% Doesn't change, ~35% Decreases.

factors.description: Post-spike decay trajectory: massive spike to 100 on Mar 12 (Trump endorsement), decaying 100→39→25→15→13. Still pointing downward toward pre-spike baseline of 7-8.
Pre-spike baseline was 7-8 (early March avg ~7.7), below the Decreases threshold of <10. Full reversion means Decreases.
The Trump endorsement / 'running for office' narrative is developing, not a one-time event. Rising queries (212k+ volume) suggest sustained political interest keeping values elevated.
Full-period change stats show 72.7% no-change rate with ±3 threshold, suggesting stability is the default outcome.
Decay rate slowing dramatically (ratio 0.39→0.64→0.60→0.87). Value may plateau around 10-12 rather than returning to 7-8 floor.
No specific major Jake Paul event scheduled for Mar 17-24. He's recovering from jaw surgery until late 2026. A value >16 requires a significant new catalyst.
Monte Carlo simulations with baseline ~9.6 show: Increases ~9%, Doesn't change ~55%, Decreases ~35%. Sensitivity to baseline assumption is high.
SerpAPI measurement uncertainty of ±1-2 points affects borderline outcomes near the 10-point threshold between Decreases and Doesn't change.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Jake Paul Google Trends Interest (Mar 16-24)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Increases | 10% |
| Doesn't change | 50% |
| Decreases | 40% |

**Key reasoning:**
- **Start value: 13** on Mar 16, post-spike decay from a massive 100 on Mar 12 (Trump endorsement/political office news)
- **Threshold arithmetic:** Increases >16, Decreases <10, Doesn't change covers [10, 16]
- **Decay dynamics:** The decay is clearly slowing (daily ratios: 0.39→0.64→0.60→0.87), suggesting the value is approaching an equilibrium — but whether that equilibrium is 10-12 (Doesn't change) or 7-8 (Decreases) is the central uncertainty
- **Developing narrative:** "Jake paul running for office" and "Trump endorses jake paul" are massive rising queries, which could sustain interest above the pre-spike floor
- **No scheduled catalyst** for the window, making Increases unlikely without surprise news
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “jake paul” change between 2026-03-16 and 2026-03-24 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42684)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=jake%20paul&date=2026-02-22%202026-03-24](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=jake%20paul&date=2026-02-22%202026-03-24) for 2026-03-24 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-16. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "jake paul",
  "date": "2026-02-22 2026-03-24",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
